summary: Backend testing completed for POST /api/social/upload-media endpoint. All 9 tests PASSED (100%). The endpoint correctly uploads images/videos to Cloudinary and returns valid https://res.cloudinary.com/... URLs instead of blob:// URLs. This fixes the Facebook/Instagram API errors.
seed_data_creation: Test images uploaded to Cloudinary during tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: The /api/social/upload-media endpoint is fully functional. It uploads media to Cloudinary and returns public URLs. The frontend SocialComposer.jsx has been updated to call this endpoint instead of using URL.createObjectURL() which created unusable blob:// URLs.

tests: Response contains metadata (format, width, height, bytes)
URL format is correct Cloudinary pattern

File Type Validation.details: Only image/* and video/* content types accepted

File Size Validation.details: 10MB limit for images, 50MB limit for videos

Cloudinary Integration.details: Files uploaded to 'social_media_posts' folder with unique public_id

url_accessibility.details: Cloudinary URL returns HTTP 200 with correct content-type

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Images were stored as blob:// URLs which caused Facebook '(#100) url should represent a valid URL' and Instagram 'Only photo or video can be accepted as media type' errors
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: MediaUploader in SocialComposer.jsx now calls /api/social/upload-media which uploads to Cloudinary and returns public https:// URLs
bug_fix_verification.verification: Endpoint returns https://res.cloudinary.com/... URLs that are publicly accessible by Meta APIs
